2606.31602.title: Robust Text Watermarking for Large Language Models via Dual Semantic Embeddings
2606.31602.paper_summary: This work presents Dual-Embedding Watermarking (DEW), a semantic watermarking scheme for large language models (LLMs) that leverages contextual and token-level embeddings to enhance robustness against paraphrasing and translation. DEW utilizes a signal-processing methodology, applying algebraic vector-space operations to \mbox{token and context embeddings to derive a watermark signal that degrades gracefully under semantic shifts. The method obfuscates the watermark by projecting embedding vectors through pseudo-random matrices seeded with a secret key. Relevant distributions derived from the underlying algebra are evaluated and employed for statistical testing and benchmarking of DEW. Experimental results across multiple LLMs indicate that DEW improves post-paraphrase detection while maintaining competitive text quality, and remains detectable after translation, even when prior semantic watermarks degrade significantly. These findings position DEW as a practical and robust solution for safeguarding LLM-generated text and addressing critical issues in responsible AI deployment.
2606.31602.paper_summary_zh: 這項工作提出了雙嵌入水印（DEW），這是一種針對大型語言模型（LLMs）的語義水印方案，利用上下文和標記級別的嵌入來增強對改寫和翻譯的魯棒性。<lb/>DEW採用信號處理方法，通過代數向量空間操作應用於標記和上下文嵌入，以推導出在語義變化下能夠優雅降級的水印信號。<lb/>該方法通過通過以秘密鑰匙為種子的偽隨機矩陣投影嵌入向量來混淆水印。<lb/>從基礎代數中導出的相關分佈被評估並用於DEW的統計測試和基準測試。<lb/>在多個LLM上的實驗結果表明，DEW在保持競爭性文本質量的同時改善了改寫後的檢測，並且在翻譯後仍然可檢測，即使先前的語義水印顯著降級。<lb/>這些發現使DEW成為保護LLM生成文本的實用和魯棒的解決方案，並解決負責任的AI部署中的關鍵問題。
2606.31602.author: Jonas Schäfer et.al.
2606.31602.authors: Jonas Schäfer, Cezary Pilaszewicz, Gerhard Wunder
